title: Roles and responsibilities of Data engineer working with Apache Zeppelin and other big data frameworks

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Roles and responsibilities of Data engineer working with Apache Zeppelin and other big data frameworks

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As a Data Engineer, working with Apache Zeppelin and other big data frameworks, there are several roles and responsibilities that you are expected to undertake. Some of these responsibilities include:

1. Design, develop and implement big data solutions using Apache Zeppelin and other big data frameworks to meet business requirements.

2. Work with data architects, data analysts, data scientists, and other stakeholders to understand business requirements and translate them into technical requirements.

3. Develop and maintain data pipelines, ETL processes and data manipulation operations to extract, transform and load data from multiple sources into a big data platform.

4. Optimize data storage, processing, and retrieval to ensure timely delivery of data and reports to users.

5. Ensure data quality and integrity in the processing of data by performing data cleansing and validation.

6. Analyze big data to generate valuable insights and recommendations for business users.

7. Develop and implement data security measures to protect sensitive data from cyber threats and data breaches.

8. Monitor and troubleshoot performance issues in the big data platform to ensure uninterrupted data processing and delivery.

9. Stay updated with the latest big data technologies and best practices to continuously improve the big data platform.

10. Develop and maintain technical documentation to ensure effective knowledge sharing and transfer between team members.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Roles and responsibilities of Technical Specialist working in Data Science Enablement Team at NatWest Group

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As a Technical Specialist working in the Data Science Enablement Team at NatWest Group, there are several roles and responsibilities that you are expected to undertake. Some of these responsibilities include:

1. Building and maintaining data science platforms and tools to support data scientists with their work.

2. Providing technical expertise to data scientists, enabling them to extract insights and value from data using advanced analytical techniques.

3. Working closely with business stakeholders to understand their requirements and identify opportunities to use data science to drive business outcomes.

4. Developing and deploying machine learning models, including data preparation, feature engineering, model selection and tuning, and deployment frameworks.

5. Collaborating with other technical teams to ensure the successful integration of the data science platform with existing systems and databases.

6. Ensuring that data science work meets relevant regulatory and compliance requirements, including data privacy and ethical considerations.

7. Managing and maintaining infrastructure, including cloud computing environments and data storage and retrieval systems.

8. Supporting the development of data science skills within the wider organization, including training and mentoring of other team members.

9. Staying on top of the latest developments and trends in data science and technology, and contributing to the wider data science community.

10. Demonstrating excellence in problem solving through innovative and creative solutions, and contributing to continuous improvement and optimization of existing systems, platforms and processes.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Roles and responsibilities of Technical Specialist working in Data Engineering team

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As a Technical Specialist working in the Data Engineering team, there are several roles and responsibilities that you are expected to undertake. Some of these responsibilities include:

1. Build, test, and maintain large-scale, high-performance data processing systems, including data pipelines, data storage, and data retrieval systems.

2. Work closely with data analysts, data scientists, and other stakeholders to understand business requirements and translate them into technical requirements.

3. Develop and maintain ETL processes for ingesting data from a wide range of sources and transforming it into a format suitable for analysis and reporting.

4. Optimize data storage, processing, and retrieval to ensure timely delivery of data and reports to business users.

5. Ensure data quality and integrity in the processing of data by performing data cleansing and validation.

6. Monitor and troubleshoot performance issues in the data processing and delivery systems.

7. Develop and implement data security measures to protect sensitive data from cyber threats and data breaches.

8. Stay up to date with the latest big data technologies and best practices to continuously improve the data processing and delivery systems.

9. Collaborate with other technical teams, including data scientists and software developers, to ensure the successful integration of data processing and analysis systems with existing applications and platforms.

10. Develop and maintain technical documentation to ensure effective knowledge sharing and transfer between team members.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Roles and responsibilities of Technical Specialist working in cloud operations in Data Science Enablement Team

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As a Technical Specialist working in cloud operations in the Data Science Enablement Team, there are several roles and responsibilities that you are expected to undertake. Some of these responsibilities include:

1. Build and maintain cloud infrastructure that supports data science, including data storage, computing environments, and security.

2. Ensure the availability, scalability and reliability of the cloud infrastructure by monitoring and optimizing performance and managing incidents and alerts.

3. Work closely with the data science team to understand their requirements and provide technical solutions that meet their needs.

4. Collaborate with other technical teams, including data engineering, software development, and IT operations, to ensure the successful integration of cloud infrastructure with existing systems and databases.

5. Develop and maintain technical documentation for the cloud infrastructure and processes to ensure effective knowledge sharing and transfer between team members.

6. Provide technical expertise and guidance to team members and stakeholders on cloud computing technologies and best practices.

7. Ensure the security of the cloud infrastructure by maintaining compliance with relevant regulatory and compliance requirements, including data privacy and ethical considerations.

8. Manage and maintain cloud infrastructure costs by monitoring and optimizing resource utilization.

9. Stay up-to-date with the latest cloud computing technologies and best practices to continuously improve the cloud infrastructure.

10. Demonstrate excellence in problem-solving through innovative and creative solutions, and contribute to continuous improvement and optimization of existing systems, platforms, and processes.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Roles and responsibilities of FinOps Practioner

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
As a FinOps Practitioner, your roles and responsibilities are focused on managing the financial aspects of cloud computing. Some of these roles and responsibilities include:

1. Monitoring and controlling cloud computing costs by analyzing usage patterns, identifying inefficiencies, and optimizing resource utilization.

2. Collaborating with stakeholders, including IT teams, Finance, and Procurement, to develop and enforce cloud financial management policies and governance.

3. Providing cost visibility and financial insights to stakeholders through extensive reporting insights and analysis.

4. Continuously evaluating cloud infrastructure usage patterns and cost structure to align with business goals and process.

5. Providing guidance to stakeholders on budgeting, forecasting, and cost optimization strategies, reducing wasteful spending and increasing the ROI.

6. Analyzing and interpreting data and metrics to identify opportunities for cost savings while increasing efficiency and effectiveness of operations.

7. Developing and implementing financial models used for trending, forecasting, and what-if analysis for IT budgeting.

8. Collaborating with IT teams involved in monitoring, provisioning, and managing cloud assets to ensure that operation efficiencies are maximized.

9. Ensuring compliance with budgetary targets, fiscal regulations, and overall cloud financial objectives.

10. Tracking the cost savings achieved with different initiatives and data-driven recommendations to senior stakeholders on how to improve the FinOps system or add more advanced analytics.
